issue_tracker_summary.note: All 5 critical and 25 major issues already have patches written (status=patched). 2 new minor issues (ISS-546, ISS-547) added as 'open' from WIN-025 Cycle 2. 1 new moderate issue (ISS-545) added as 'patched' from WIN-026 Cycle 2 with patch written this run. 23 patch files awaiting human application.

new_issues_this_run.description: WIN-026 Cycle 2: Internal contradiction not exploited — dome's 05-model.txt labels 5,733 km sun altitude as '(optical illusion due to aetheric refraction)' but WIN-026 claims ray geometry confirms a physical sun at this altitude

suggested_patch.find: consistent with a sun at 150 million km.
suggested_patch.replace: consistent with a sun at 150 million km. Notably, the dome model's own parameter documentation (raw-text/05-model.txt) labels the 5,733 km figure as "(optical illusion due to aetheric refraction)" — conceding that WIN-026's claimed confirmation of a physically local sun at this altitude is internally inconsistent: the model characterises 5,733 km as a perceptual artefact of aetheric refraction, not an actual physical location for the sun. A claimed WIN that relies on geometry-at-5,733-km cannot simultaneously treat that altitude as an optical illusion.

new_issues_this_run.description: WIN-025 Cycle 2: Missing framing that standard physics predicts eclipse response for ANY eclipse, not just 2024 — pre-empts cherry-picking defense
WIN-025 Cycle 2: Missing direct Chernogor & Holub attribution quote for standard Sq current suppression

under_covered_digest_analysis.notes: The digest reported 221 holes in 61 under-covered processed reviews. After systematic verification against current wins.json, the vast majority of these holes were already fixed in previous decider/analyst runs. Key resolutions confirmed: WIN-033 finding (correct distances 433ly/294ly already present), WIN-047 z-range (z~8 already in text), WIN-052 DOI (ad54b0 already correct), WIN-053 verdict (Self-Contradicted already correct, garbled sentence already fixed), WIN-055 Cepheid framing (already says 'not direct geometry'), WIN-057 claim/finding (already cross-equatorial focused), WIN-058 all three major holes (current text already has stronger argument about post-hoc fitting, not weak convention argument), WIN-059 killshot argument (already in text), WIN-066 DOI (ngeo1580 is correct, already fixed), WIN-067 satellite context (already says 'well below firmament'). The curmudgeon reviews were mostly reviewing older text versions.

Apply manual changes for EXP-007 (tab reorder in generate-html.js) and EXP-011 (DOCX parity in build-doc-v4.js) — these cannot be auto-applied
After patch application, run node build.js publish to push to GitHub Pages
